Very interesting, this girl actually presented with difficulty in walking down the stairs.
So why would I dig into her chest if she couldn't walk down the stairs?
Well friends, she couldn't walk down the stairs because of a condition known as myasthenia gravis.
Myasthenia gravis means a very severe and grave weakness of the muscles.
Why were her muscles weak?
Because inside this thymus there was a tumor that was releasing some special antibodies.
What were those antibodies doing?
They were attacking her junctions of nerves and muscles, the so-called neuromuscular junction, and making her very, very weak.
Therefore, the thymoma or tumor in the thymus had to be removed along with the rest of the thymus.
The surgeon uses a minimally invasive approach with three small incisions in the chest to access the thymus, avoiding a traditional open chest procedure.
The surgeon uses ultrasonic shears through the thoracic ports to dissect tissue within the chest cavity, focusing on the removal of the thymus and associated thymoma.
The surgeon uses ultrasonic shears to cut tissue, utilizing ultrasound energy to avoid burning the tissue during the thymectomy procedure.
The surgeon gently dissects the tumor and thymus, carefully shelling out the tumor from surrounding tissue using precise movements.
